plainEnglish: For pre-recorded video with audio, give people who can't see the screen a way to get the visual information: either an audio description track that narrates important visual details during pauses, or a full text alternative (a document describing both audio and visuals). This covers things shown on screen but never spoken aloud.
whyItMatters: Blind and low-vision users miss on-screen information—like text, charts, or actions that aren't spoken—when a video has no audio description or text alternative.
quickCheck: Watch the video with your eyes closed: if you lose track of important visual information, it needs an audio description track or a full text alternative.
commonMistakes: Assuming captions are enough—captions help deaf users, but blind users need the visuals described.
Skipping audio description because 'the narrator explains everything,' without verifying on-screen text and actions are covered.
Providing a transcript of only the dialogue, omitting any description of the visual content.

codeExamples.label: Video with both captions and an audio-description track
codeExamples.code:
<video controls src="/tutorial.mp4">
  <track kind="captions" src="/tutorial.en.vtt" srcLang="en" label="English" default />
  <track kind="descriptions" src="/tutorial.desc.vtt" srcLang="en" label="Audio description" />
</video>

codeExamples.label: Video with captions only, no description of visuals
codeExamples.code:
<video controls src="/tutorial.mp4">
  <track kind="captions" src="/tutorial.en.vtt" srcLang="en" label="English" default />
</video>
